methodology: secret scan (env-only key sourcing — no hardcoded admin/signer keys)
injection scan (no shell=True, no eval/exec, list-form subprocess args, JSON parse via serde)
xss scan (React rendering, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, toast uses text content)
access control (admin endpoints require BRIDGE_ADMIN_KEY via constant-time compare in both Python and Rust)
crypto review (schnorrkel sr25519 with substrate context + <Bytes> wrapper fallback; ed25519-dalek for Solana; SS58 checksum validated on decode)
replay protection (per-claim and per-commit signature digests stored in used_signatures.json; 5-minute timestamp window for claim sigs)
rate limiting (per-IP sliding window, X-Forwarded-For trusted only from loopback so Caddy passes real client IP without external spoof risk)
DoS surface (8KB body limit; rate-limited /balances and /get_total_balances; paged snapshot loader on the frontend)
data integrity (atomic file writes via tempfile + persist; in-memory state guarded by parking_lot RwLock)
info leakage (errors are JSON {error:string}; no stack traces; admin token never logged)

fixes_applied_this_review.issue: Python admin token comparison used `==` (timing leak)
Rate limiter saw proxy IP (127.0.0.1) when running behind Caddy
fixes_applied_this_review.fix: client_ip() reads X-Forwarded-For but only when the connection originates from loopback — external clients can't spoof

fixes_applied_this_review.issue: /get_total_balances returned the entire 6MB snapshot with no rate limit (DoS surface)
fixes_applied_this_review.fix: added rate limiter to /get_total_balances; frontend migrated to paged /balances?page=&limit= so the legacy endpoint sees minimal traffic

fixes_applied_this_review.issue: Python API was the production server with weaker validation/replay surface
fixes_applied_this_review.fix: replaced with Rust axum service: explicit length+charset validation, constant-time compare, atomic JSON writes, schnorrkel-based sr25519 verification, body size limit
